transcript.message: AI audio recording enabled
Interview session connected. Microphone is ready.
Audio recording started
Microphone enabled. You can start speaking.
Interview started. Please introduce yourself and share what drew you to caregiving work.
Bye.
Hi, I'm CareBot. This takes about 12 minutes. Short examples help. Please avoid private patient details. Ready?
Yes
Great, let's start. Have you ever worked as a caregiver? If so, what kinds of patients did you work with and what services did you perform?

Sure, yes, I have worked for about two years as a home caregiver supporting mostly seniors and a few post-operative clients. Day to day I handle bathing, dressing, light meal prep, basic mobility support and checking vitals. I also did light housekeeping.
Thanks for sharing. Now, imagine you're finding a caregiver for a loved one. What traits or skills would be most important?
Patients are a liability for sure. I'd want someone who communicates clearly, follows the care plan and respects routines.
basic safety awareness as well and being calm under pressure matters too.
Got it. Now, how has your prior work or life experience helped you build the skills to be a good caregiver?
The last role taught me to stay organized and follow instructions from nurses and families.
and I can prioritize when several tasks compete and stay calm when a client is having a tough day.
Got it. Why do you want to be a caregiver? What are the most rewarding aspects of the job for you?
I like helping people feel safe at home. The best part is when a client is comfortable and you can tell you made their day easier.
That makes sense. Tell me about a time when being late caused problems for you or your team. What did you learn?
I was late once because a bus broke down, but my client missed a short walk, but I apologized when I arrived, and after that, I started leaving earlier.
Thanks for sharing that. Have you ever adjusted your routine to ensure punctuality at work? What changes did you make?
Yeah
What changes did

I mean I prepped clothes night and set two alarms beforehand and I checked my the morning route before I move out I check the route on my phone which has helped me to be on more time more consistently.
Good strategy. Now, tell me about a time when you cared for a senior. What was most difficult, and what was most meaningful?

I supported a client with memory issues. The hardest part was answering the same questions repeatedly without losing calm. But I managed to give him simple reassuring answers and keep a steady routine.
That made her relax a lot
It sounds like you made a real difference. Now, tell me about a time you helped a colleague or client who was struggling emotionally. What did you do?
Our coworker was overwhelmed during a busy week. I asked privately if they were OK. I listened for a few minutes, and then offered to swap a task so they could finish relaxing. That seemed to help.
Empathy goes a long way. Lastly, if we asked your coworkers about you, what three things would they say?
They would say that I am dependable, polite and let's say impatient as well.
Thanks for sharing. We'll review and update you soon. Please leave the meeting by clicking the button below. Take care.
